text: Vessel energy dissection is a simulated tissue exercise which requires you to dissect out a vessel, coagulate at two ends, proximal and distal, and transect in the middle of those coagulation points. Similar to the energy pedal exercises, your left hand has a grasper that does bipolar coagulation, and the right hand has scissors that can do monopolar cut and coagulation. You can use your left hand grasper to grab the fatty tissue and create tension, which allows you to more easily dissect out the sides of the vessel wall. And care should be taken with the soft tissue dissection not to go too close to the vessel as to risk cutting or injuring it, which will result in a penalty or a critical failure.

The screen displays instructions to dissect through the fat layer to expose the vessel wall. The surgeon uses scissors to scrape off residual fatty tissue from the anterior surface of the vessel without energy application. Once the vessel is exposed, the surgeon uses a grasper to perform bipolar coagulation at two indicated points until the vessel turns white, signaling satisfactory coagulation. The exercise then prompts cutting the vessel between the coagulation points, while avoiding injury or excessive tension.
